commodore:
The Commodore - Live Music & Jazz Club

Address: 504 Meeting Street, Charleston, SC 29403

Hours:
Sunday - Wednesday: 7:30 PM - 1:00 AM
Thursday - Saturday: 7:30 PM - 2:00 AM

Age Requirement: 23 & Up

What to Expect: Vintage jazz club that started in the early 80s as "Touch of Class." Unparalleled live music and bar scene with a throwback-meets-modern atmosphere, fantastic dance floor, and craft cocktails.

Live Music: Up to 5 nights a week — Lady & The Brass, The Majestics, The Funktastics, Future Funk, plus touring acts.

Bach Tips: No formal packages but the dance floor + live funk/jazz + craft cocktails = ideal group night. 23+ age policy keeps it mature. Arrive early on weekends.
sip-history:
Sip History - Cocktail Classes

Location: Hosted at Frontier Lounge, downtown Charleston

Options:
• Sip History in The Sun (outdoor) — $87/person
• Sip History in a Secret Speakeasy (indoor) — $87/person

What's Included: 2-hour hands-on class, 3 craft cocktails, light snacks, all ingredients & equipment, recipes to take home. No experience needed.

Reviews: Over 4,000 verified 5-star reviews across 6 platforms — one of the highest-rated activities in Charleston.

Bach Tips: Private bookings available for groups. Classes fill fast for weekend bachelorette groups — book well in advance.

Cancellation: Full refund 24+ hours before. Inside 24 hours: non-refundable.
bloom-spa:
Bloom Wellness Spa - Bachelorette Packages

Group Size: 2-12 guests
All packages include: Champagne toast (non-alcoholic available), spa robes, sandals, spa waters, locker room with showers.

Packages:
• Pamper (Bestseller): $185/pp — 60-min massage with hot towels, sauna, champagne + snacks
• Spa & Wellness Day: $249/pp — massage, detox sauna, saltwater float, champagne + snacks
• Detox & Glo: $275/pp — custom massage, infrared sauna, express facial, champagne + snacks
• Ultimate Spa Day: $499/pp — massage + hot stones, facial, sauna, float therapy, group event (sound bowls/yoga), catered food + premium champagne

Fine Print: Must be 21+ for champagne. Arrive 10 min early. Free cancellation 24+ hours before.

Tip: Most groups choose the Pamper package — under $200/guest and crowd-pleasing.
hampton-park:
Hampton Park - Charleston's Largest Park

Location: Upper peninsula, near The Citadel
Hours: Dawn to dusk daily | Free

What to Expect: Paved 1-mile loop through manicured gardens, rose gardens, fountains, and mature live oaks. Flat and easy — great for a casual morning walk or jog.

Photo Spots: The bandstand and rose garden are especially scenic.

Amenities: Free street parking, restrooms, benches, picnic areas. Pets on leash welcome.

Bach Tips: Perfect for a relaxed morning walk + group photos among the gardens. Combine with brunch at a nearby King Street restaurant. No reservations needed.
waterfront-park:
Waterfront Park - Charleston Harbor

Address: Concord Street, downtown Charleston
Phone: (843) 724-7324
Hours: Sunrise to sunset daily | Free

Features:
• 0.50-mile paved path (brick, boardwalk)
• Famous Pineapple Fountain (the most Instagrammable spot in CHS)
• Two illuminated fountains (spectacular after dark)
• Harbor-facing swings
• Free Wi-Fi
• On-site parking

Bach Tips: The Pineapple Fountain is THE group photo spot. Harbor swings are great for relaxing. Visit at sunset for golden-hour photos. Walking distance to downtown restaurants and bars.
charles-towne:
Charles Towne Landing State Historic Site

Address: 1500 Old Towne Road, Charleston, SC 29407
Phone: (843) 852-4200

Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM daily
Admission: $12/adult | $7.50/SC Senior | $7/youth

What to See:
• 12-room interactive exhibit hall
• The Adventure — 17th-century replica ship you can board
• Animal Forest — the only zoo in Charleston (otters, bears, bison)
• 80 acres of gardens with live oak alley
• 6 miles of trails

Bach Tips: The live oak alley and Legare-Waring House make gorgeous photo backdrops. Great morning nature outing before heading downtown. Allow 2-3 hours.

Amenities: Wi-Fi, EV charging, pets allowed outdoors on leash.
west-ashley-greenway:
West Ashley Greenway Trail

Length: 8.2 miles | Flat, paved asphalt (gravel at western end)

Access Points:
• Eastern: Albemarle Road at Croghan Spur Road
• Western: 3484 McLeod Mill Road
Parking: South Windermere shopping center, 25 Lyttleton Ave, McLeod Mill Road

Highlights: Western 1.4 miles feature a causeway along marsh and creek — popular for crabbing, fishing, and coastal bird sightings (egrets, herons). Part of the East Coast Greenway.

Important: No restrooms or water fountains. Bring water!

Bach Tips: Best for active groups. Rent bikes and ride to the scenic marsh section. Morning visits recommended to avoid July heat.
sc-aquarium:
South Carolina Aquarium

Address: 100 Aquarium Wharf, Charleston, SC 29401
Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM daily

What to See: Thousands of animals, Sea Turtle Care Center, touch tanks, VR experiences, and daily programs.

Special: "Aquarium For Adults" programming — check events calendar for evening adult-only events with cocktails.

Tickets: Buy online at scaquarium.org for early-bird discounts. Group visit options available.

Bach Tips: Adult-only evening events are ideal for a unique bachelorette outing. Harbor views outside are great for photos. Walking distance to Waterfront Park and downtown restaurants.
the-battery:
The Battery & White Point Garden

Location: Southern tip of the peninsula, where East Bay meets Murray Blvd
Hours: Open 24 hours | Free

Highlights:
• Sweeping harbor views, Fort Sumter, and Ravenel Bridge
• Civil War cannons and monuments
• Victorian gazebo — popular for photos
• Massive live oaks with Spanish moss
• Some of Charleston's most photographed antebellum mansions

Bach Tips: Walk the promenade at sunset for golden-hour photos with mansions and harbor behind you. Combine with Rainbow Row (few blocks north on East Bay). Completely free, open anytime. Street parking on Murray Blvd.
harbor-bar:
Harbor Bar - Bachelorette Party Boat

Address: Ripley Light Marina, 56 Ashley Point Drive, Charleston, SC 29407
Phone: (843) 810-5912

What's Included:
• 1 hour 45 min cruise around Charleston Harbor
• Up to 26 guests, BYOB
• Bluetooth stereo & party lighting
• Bathroom, coolers with ice, Polaroid photos
• Dolphins frequently spotted!

Pricing:
• Public booze cruise: $45/pp weekdays, $55/pp weekends
• Private charter: from $625
• Add-ons: catering, live sushi, bartender service

Cancellation: Full reschedule/refund if weather is bad, no questions asked.

Tip: Book online at harborbarchs.com. Private charter lets you customize playlists and bring decorations.
cocktail-co:
Charleston Cocktail Co

Email: hello@charlestoncocktail.co

What's Included:
• Prosecco toast to start
• 3 curated cocktails with Jack Rudy Cocktail Co ingredients
• All supplies, tools, ingredients provided
• Optional cocktail competition add-on ($20/pp extra)
• ~2 hours

Pricing: $85/pp, minimum $850/class (~10 person min). Mocktails: $50/pp.

Booking: Submit inquiry on website, 48-72hr response. 50% deposit secures date, remainder due 1 week before.

Cancellation: 4+ months: full refund. Within 4 months: deposit non-refundable. Within 2 weeks: no refund.

Tip: They come to your rental — you provide the space, they bring everything else!
bulldog-ghost:
Bulldog Tours - Ghost, Food & History Tours

Address: 18 Anson Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: 843-722-8687
Rating: 4.6/5 stars

Tour Options:
• Ghost Tours — haunted sites, cemeteries, Charleston Haunted Jail
• Food Tours — progressive supper, grits, she-crab soup & BBQ
• Upper King Street Cocktail Tour — great for bachelorettes!
• Bachelorette Bar Crawl — handpicked venues from pubs to speakeasies
• History Tours — secret gardens, cobblestone streets

Group Options: Private, customized walking tours. Can assist with dining and event planning.

Tip: The Bachelorette Bar Crawl or Upper King Cocktail Tour are the best picks for a bach weekend.
poogans-porch:
Poogan's Porch - Southern Restaurant

Address: 72 Queen Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 829-4332

Hours: Brunch daily 9 AM - 3 PM. Dinner service available.

Brunch Highlights:
• Award-Winning Shrimp & Grits (USA Today featured)
• Bone-In Fried Chicken ($24)
• Chicken & Waffles with bourbon huckleberry syrup ($22)
• Crab Cake Benedict ($25)
• Mimosa carafes ($36) & Bloody Marys ($12)

Desserts: Banana Pudding ($10), Cast-Iron Peach Cobbler ($12)

Tip: A Charleston institution since 1976. Make reservations in advance, especially for weekend brunch. Private events & catering available.
republic-lounge:
Republic Garden & Lounge

Address: 462 King Street, Charleston, SC 29403

What to Expect:
• DJ-driven nightclub with dance floor
• Indoor/outdoor spaces including a garden area
• Craft cocktails and bottle service
• Upper King Street — heart of the nightlife district

Bach Tips: Great late-night stop during a bar crawl. Inquire about VIP/bottle service for groups. Check Instagram (@republiccharleston) for events and DJ lineups.
uptown-social:
Uptown Social

Address: 587 King Street, Charleston, SC 29403
Phone: (843) 793-1837

Hours: Mon-Thu 12PM-2AM, Fri-Sun 11:30AM-2AM

Features:
• 48 TVs, live music every night, DJs
• Iconic "Social Butterfly" mural — perfect for group photos!
• 12" bar pies by Brooklyn pizza legend Anthony Falco
• Private event rooms available
• Skip the line via LineLeap app

Tip: Great for groups wanting food, drinks, and dancing all in one spot. Make reservations online for groups.
earthling-spa:
Earthling Day Spa - Group Packages

Address: 245 East Bay Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 722-4737

Hours: Mon-Fri 9AM-7PM, Sat 9AM-5PM, Sun 12PM-5PM

Services:
• Massage: Therapeutic ($120-$300), Aromatherapy, Hot Stone, Deep Tissue, Thai, Cupping
• Facials, Body Treatments, Manicure & Pedicure
• Waxing, Lash Lift and Tint
• Add-ons: Scalp Massage ($35-$70), Steam session ($55)

Group Booking: Fill out form on website or call. Provide each person's name, services desired, and therapist gender preference. Response within 24 hours.

Tip: Book early — group slots fill fast on weekends. Pilates studio also available onsite.
tiki-tours:
Tiki Tours of Charleston

Phone: 843-990-8454
Reviews: 465 five-star Google reviews

The Boat: "Friki Tiki" — 32-passenger floating tiki island, USCG captain & crew.

What's Included: BYOB (no hard liquor), coolers/ice/water, Bluetooth sound, cups/tables/limbo/games, restroom, floating mats for swim stops (May-Sept).

Pricing:
• Semi-Private: from $400/hr ($80-$120/pp)
• Private Charter: $700-$900/hr (2hr min)

Add-Ons: Party bus, live DJ ($300/2hrs), bartender ($200/2hrs), e-foil ($250), catering/charcuterie.

Special: Morning Mimosa Cruise (BYOB champagne, OJ included).

Cancellation: 30+ days: full refund. 3-30 days: reschedule. Within 72hrs: non-refundable. Weather cancel: full refund.

Tip: Gratuity 15-20% recommended for crew. Dolphins spotted on 90% of cruises!
blind-tiger:
Blind Tiger Pub

Address: 36-38 Broad Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 577-0088

What to Expect:
• Beautiful outdoor courtyard — one of the best patios in Charleston
• Historic building dating to 1803
• Full bar with craft cocktails and local beers
• Casual, laid-back atmosphere
• No cover charge

Bach Tips: Gorgeous courtyard for group photos. Perfect first stop or afternoon drinks before heading to King Street nightlife. Courtyard fills up on weekends — arrive early.
junction-brunch:
The Junction Kitchen & Provisions

Address: 4438 Spruill Ave, Park Circle, Charleston, SC 29405
Phone: (843) 745-9189

About: Solo female-owned neighborhood gem. One of the only full-service breakfast joints in the area. American eclectic cuisine with bold flavors and generous portions.

Menu Highlights: Smokey shrimp & grits, chicken & waffles, blackberry mimosas, creative brunch cocktails.

Private Events: Offers private event space for parties. Inquire through catering/events page.

Note: Located in Park Circle (North Charleston), about 15 min from downtown — not walking distance. Worth the trip for an off-the-beaten-path brunch.
undiscovered-chs:
Charleston Tours (Tour of Charleston)

Address: 207 E. Bay St, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 284-3668

Tours:
• History & Architecture Tour
• Pirates and Ghosts Tour
• Historic Food Tour
• Craft Beer & Cocktails Tour
• Bachelorette Bar Crawl — handpicked venues from cozy pubs to hidden speakeasies

Tip: The Bachelorette Bar Crawl or Craft Beer & Cocktails tour is the best fit — fun, social, and a great way to discover Charleston's nightlife together.
head-spa:
Charleston Head Spa — Group Booking

About: Japanese-inspired head spa treatments including scalp analysis, deep cleansing, scalp massage, hair treatment, and relaxation techniques.

Group Booking: Dedicated group booking page for bachelorette parties. Visit website to submit inquiry with party size and date.

Why It's Great: Head spas are a trending wellness experience — unique, relaxing, and Instagram-worthy. Something most guests haven't tried, making it memorable. Perfect chill morning/afternoon between high-energy activities.

Tip: Check Instagram (@charlestonheadspa) for latest photos, pricing, and availability.
king-street:
King Street Shopping — Downtown Charleston

Shopping by Block:
• 114-198 King: Antiques, Art Galleries (The Impeccable Pig at 175)
• 200-299 King: The Shops at Charleston Place (luxury retail)
• 300-388 King: Hampden Women's Clothing (314 King)
• 401-495 King: Two Cumberland (women's clothing, jewelry, gifts)
• 501-700 King: Art, Clothing, Interior Design

Bach Highlights: The Impeccable Pig for trendy group shopping, Hampden for curated fashion, Two Cumberland for matching accessories.

Tip: Most stores open ~10am. The street is fully walkable with restaurants for breaks. Free to browse!
party-cat:
Charleston Party Cat

Pickup: 8 Ashley Point Dr, dock next to California Dreaming sign, Slip #D7
Email: info@charlestonpartycat.com

Boat Options: Party Cat, Karaoke Cat, Lux Cat, Disco Darty, Karaoke Grande, Cowgirl Beach Club — multiple vibes and sizes.

What's Included: Ice, water, coolers, restrooms, drinking games, large floats, sound system, experienced crew.

Duration: 2hr minimum. 3hrs lets you swim with floats. BYOB-friendly.

Tip: The Karaoke Cat or Disco Darty boats add extra fun factor! Book early for peak weekends.
cocktail-crew:
Cocktail Crew — Private Cocktail Classes

Phone: 602-584-5148 | As Seen On: Shark Tank

How It Works: Inquire → Reserve date + pick 2-3 cocktails for 8-14 guests → They send a shopping list for alcohol & ice → Bartender arrives, sets up, leads 60-90 min class → Full cleanup.

Pricing: $55/pp (2 cocktails), $75/pp (3 cocktails) + 6% booking fee. You buy the alcohol.

Menus: The Locals (Planter's Punch & Cadillac Marg), The Martinis, The Classics (PB&J Old Fashioned), The Mocktails, The Spritzes.

Reviews: 500+ events, 5-star reviews.

Tip: Perfect at your rental house! Choose the 3-cocktail option for more variety. Dietary restrictions and mocktails accommodated.
divadance:
DivaDance — Charleston

About: Pop-music choreography classes for all skill levels. No dance experience needed — learn a full routine to Beyonce, Britney, Lizzo, etc.

Private Events: Private group dance classes available. Choose your music vibe. Most popular bachelorette offering nationwide.

What to Expect: High-energy, laughter-filled dance session learning a choreographed routine. Great for photos and videos!

Tip: Wear comfortable clothes and shoes. Pairs perfectly as an afternoon activity before going out. Ask about recording your final performance!
church-union:
Church & Union — Charleston

Address: 32b North Market Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 937-8666

Hours:
• Dinner: Daily 5pm-Till
• Lunch: Mon-Thu 10am-3:30pm
• Brunch: Fri-Sun 9am-3:30pm

Chefs: Top Chef Alum Jamie Lynch + Adam Hodgson

Menu Highlights: CU Tower (oysters/shrimp/lobster $116), Tuna Tartare ($23), Seared Scallops ($44), NY Strip ($60), Fried Chicken ($31), Wagyu Lettuce Wraps ($25).

Groups 12+: Credit card on file + group menu selection required. Fill out Private Dining inquiry form.

Tip: Book Friday brunch for a bach-friendly experience, or go for a splashy group dinner. Gorgeous church interior = amazing photos.
cocktail-club:
The Cocktail Club

Address: 479 King Street, Charleston, SC 29403
Phone: (843) 724-9411

Hours: Tue-Sun 5PM-2AM (Closed Mondays)

Features:
• Craft cocktails with creative, expertly made drinks
• Rooftop terrace — perfect for warm evenings
• Speakeasy-inspired interior with moody, upscale ambiance
• Private events available

Bach Tips: Go early (~5-6pm) to snag rooftop seats, then stay as energy picks up. THE spot for a bach night out in Charleston. Pairs perfectly with nearby King Street restaurants.
brittlebank-park:
Brittlebank Park

Address: 1 Brittlebank Park, Charleston, SC 29403
Hours: Dawn to dusk daily | Free

Features: Fishing pier, walking trails, picnic areas, restrooms, free parking, playground, and stunning Ashley River/Ravenel Bridge views.

Bach Tips: Great spot for golden hour group photos with river and bridge backdrop. Pack a picnic with wine and charcuterie for a low-key afternoon. Easy free parking.
carriage-ride:
Old South Carriage Company

Address: 14 Anson Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: (843) 723-9712
Hours: Daily 9am-9pm

Tours:
• Historic Carriage Tour (daytime): $50/adult
• Haunted Carriage Tour (evening): $50/adult
• Private tours for 1-4 passengers available

Reviews: 4.9/5 from 43,479+ reviews!

Tip: Use code CVB2Off at checkout to save $2/person. Family-owned since 1983. Book the evening Haunted Tour for a fun group experience.
pedal-pub:
Saltwater Cycle Party Boats

Pickup: Ripley Marina, 8 Ashley Point Dr
Phone: (843) 901-9836

Two Boats:
• Saltwater Cycle Boat — 10 pedal seats + 6 bench (16 max)
• Charleston Brew Barge Tiki Boat — 10 bar seats, dance floor, bathroom (18 max)

Pricing: Public $50-55/pp. Private Cycle: from $675. Private Tiki: from $775.
Duration: 1.5 hours. BYOB — bring ~3 drinks/person.

Reviews: 1,170+ Google reviews, excellent rating.

Tip: Specifically offers Bachelorette Party packages. Sunset cruises are especially popular. Book early!
paint-and-sip:
Painting with a Twist — Mt. Pleasant

Address: 1336 Old Georgetown Rd #106, Mount Pleasant, SC 29464
Phone: (843) 996-9707

Pricing: ~$40-42/person. BYOB!

Events: Girls Night, Theme Nights (ACOTAR, 90s), Paint Your Pet, and more.

Bachelorette: Private parties available — customize your own event with a private room and custom paintings.

Tip: Located in Mt. Pleasant (~15 min from downtown). #1 rated paint & sip franchise in the country. Book seats online or contact for private party.
halls-chophouse:
Hall's Chophouse

Address: 434 King Street, Charleston, SC 29403
Phone: (843) 727-0090

Hours:
• Dinner: Mon-Sat 4-11pm/12am, Sun 4:30-11pm
• Saturday Lunch: 11am-2:30pm
• Sunday Brunch: 10am-2pm

About: Family-owned since 2009. Dry-aged steaks, She Crab Soup, seafood tower, live music. Consistently top 10 in Charleston.

Reservations: Book via Resy. Highly recommended — tables fill fast on weekends.

Tip: Staff excels at making celebrations memorable. Expect $75-150+/person. Sunday Brunch is a great alternative if dinner reservations are tough.
stars-rooftop:
Stars Rooftop & Grill Room

Address: 495 King Street
Phone: (843) 577-0100

Hours: Dinner Sun-Sat. Brunch Sat & Sun 10am-2:30pm. Rooftop Bar Mon-Thu 3pm, Fri-Sun 12pm.

Private Events:
• Full rooftop: up to 150 seated
• Wilkinson Room: 24-75 seated (exposed brick, King St views)
• Main banquet: up to 140

Tip: Walk-in friendly at rooftop bar. Weekend brunch is perfect for bach groups. Consider booking private rooftop area for cocktail hour.
prohibition:
Prohibition — Speakeasy Bar & Restaurant

Address: 547 King Street
Phone: (843) 459-7214

Hours: Mon-Wed 4pm, Thu-Sun 11am. Happy Hour Mon-Thu 4-6pm.

Live Music:
• Sat/Sun Brunch: live bands (11am-2pm)
• Mon-Thu: rotating artists (8-11pm)
• Fri/Sat: Live DJs (10pm-2am, 23+)

Private Events: 3 bookable areas, up to 150 people. Bottle service available.

Awards: "Best Downtown Bar" and "Best Cocktails" — Charleston City Paper.

Tip: Walk-ins encouraged. Brunch with live music is a great daytime option. Late-night DJs go til 2am (must be 23+).
escape-in-60:
Escape In 60

Address: Historic Market Street, Charleston

Hours: Tue-Thu 1-9pm, Fri 1-10:30pm, Sat 10am-10:30pm, Sun 10am-9pm. Closed Mon.

6 Rooms (all 5.0 rated):
• Davy Jones' Locker — pirate ship
• The Egyptian Chamber — ancient tomb
• The Ransom — kidnapping thriller
• Roulette Me Out — Vegas casino heist
• The Witching Hour — 1675 Charleston witch
• Back to the 80s — time-travel arcade

Group Size: 2-8 per room. Groups 8+ can book multiple rooms for a competition!

Tip: Specifically caters to bach parties. Book 2 rooms and split your group for a fun race!
blu-gorilla:
Blu Gorilla Tattoo

Address: 1434 Meeting Street, Charleston, SC 29405
Phone: (843) 805-8071

About: Charleston's oldest studio. 4th generation tattooist-owned. Best Tattoo Studio 15 years running. 10 artists on staff.

Walk-ins: Welcome! Or book an appointment online with a specific artist.

Bach Tips: Matching group tattoos are a popular bachelorette activity. Browse artist portfolios online first. Consider small matching designs (infinity symbols, dates, initials) that are quick for a group.
ritual:
Ritual Rooftop Restaurant & Lounge

Address: 145 Calhoun Street, Unit 301
Phone: (843) 410-1747
Email: vip@ritualchs.com

What It Is: Rooftop restaurant by day, nightclub by night. Part of the Kenner Group.

Features: Full menu, DJ & dancing, VIP tables, private events.

Bach Tips: One of the few true nightclub options downtown. Book a VIP table for guaranteed seating and bottle service. Rooftop dinner → nightclub = easy one-stop evening.
the-watch:
The Watch Rooftop Kitchen & Spirits

Location: Atop The Restoration Hotel

Hours: Mon-Thu 11am-11pm, Fri-Sat 11am-12am, Sun 10am-9pm

Menu: Burrata & Jam, Skillet Mac & Cheese, Smoked Wings, burgers, craft cocktails. Sunday Brunch available.

Private Events:
• Dining Room: 42 seated, 60 reception
• Observatory Terrace: 16 seated, 25 standing
• Full Buyout: 150 guests, 360° views

Large Parties: 10-20 guests: email directly. 20+: fill out inquiry form.

Bach Tips: Stunning rooftop setting. Private Observatory is ideal for a celebration dinner.
mesu:
MESU — Mexican & Sushi Fusion

Address: 570 King Street
Phone: (854) 429-1011

Hours: Daily 4pm-2am. Kitchen til 10pm. Happy Hour Mon-Fri 4-6pm.

Menu: Nachos, Baja Fish Tacos, Shrimp Tempura Sushi, Street Corn, Churros. Full bar with sake & tequila cocktails.

Reviews: "Probably the best meal I had in Charleston." "Amazing vibes! Recommend with a group."

Bach Tips: Late-night kitchen + bar til 2am = perfect dinner-to-nightlife transition. Shareable plates great for groups. Heart of Upper King nightlife.
little-palm:
Little Palm — Bar & Swim Club

Address: 237 Meeting Street (at The Ryder Hotel)
Phone: (843) 414-8030

Hours: Sun-Thu 8am-10pm, Fri-Sat 8am-12am

Meals: Breakfast Mon-Sat 8-11am, Brunch Sun 8am-4pm, Lunch Mon-Sat 11am-5pm, Dinner nightly, Happy Hour Mon-Thu 4-6pm.

Features: Pool access, outdoor backyard, gallery. Seasonal cocktails and ingredient-driven dishes.

Large Parties: Up to 7: book on Resy. 8-19: email info@littlepalmbar.com. 20+: fill out large party form. Valet $10.

Bach Tips: Pool + cocktails = standout daytime activity. Chic, Instagrammable setting tailor-made for bachelorette groups.
indaco:
Indaco — Rustic Italian

Address: 526 King Street
Phone: (843) 727-1229

Hours: Dinner Sun-Thu 5-10pm, Fri-Sat 5-11pm. Brunch Sat & Sun 10am-2:30pm. Happy Hour Mon-Fri 5-7pm.

Press: Conde Nast Traveler "40 Best Restaurants in Charleston." Eater "8 Best Italian." OpenTable "12 Top Special Occasion."

Menu: Wood-fired pizzas, handmade pastas, house-made salami, Negroni on tap. Family-style available.

Bach Tips: Family-style dining ideal for sharing. Happy hour at the bar is great pre-dinner. Weekend brunch is another excellent option.
city-market:
The Historic Charleston City Market

Location: Between Meeting St and East Bay St

Hours:
• Day Market: Daily 9:30am-5pm
• Night Market: Fri & Sat 6:30pm-10:30pm (Mar-Dec)

What You'll Find: 200+ vendors — sweetgrass baskets, local art, jewelry, crafts, souvenirs, food vendors.

Night Market: Won "Charleston's Choice for Local Art" 8 years running. Largest art market in the Southeast.

Bach Tips: Browse for matching bridal party gifts or sweetgrass basket keepsakes. Night Market on Fri/Sat is more festive — great before or after dinner. Free to explore!
bangkok-karaoke:
The Bangkok Lounge — Karaoke Bar

Address: 353 King St, 2nd Floor
Hours: Nightly 5pm-2am. Karaoke starts 6pm.

Theme Nights:
• Monday: Half-Priced Punch Bowls
• Tuesday: $3 Bourbon
• Wednesday: Country Karaoke
• Thursday: Hip Hop Karaoke
• Saturday: Opens at Noon
• Sunday: 90s Karaoke

Drinks: Bangkok Punch, frozen drinks, daily specials.

Bach Tips: Top bachelorette activity in Charleston. Monday's half-priced punch bowls and Thursday's hip hop karaoke are crowd favorites. The whole bar sings along — no private room needed!
theatre-99:
Theatre 99 — Improv Comedy

Address: 280 Meeting Street, Charleston, SC 29401
Phone: 843-853-6687

Shows: Wed-Sat at 8pm
• Wednesdays: 3 acts of improv & sketch
• Thursdays: "Laugh for a Lincoln" — just $5!
• Fri/Sat: "Take the Funny and Run" flagship show

Bar (Cash Only): Local craft beers $5-7, wine $6/glass, ATM in lobby.

Fun Extras: Buy the Cast a Round ($20), Birthday Shout-out ($10) — make the bride the star!

Private Shows: Rent the theatre for a private show, or hire them at your location.

Tip: Interactive improv means the bride-to-be will likely get pulled on stage. Shows ~90 min, perfect pre-dinner activity.
jet-skis:
Tidal Wave Watersports — Jet Skis & Party Cruises

Phone: (843) 886-8456
Hours: 8am-7pm, year-round

Jet Ski Rates:
• 1 Hour: $139 single / $169 double
• 30 Min (Downtown only): $75 / $95
• All guided tours, brand new SeaDoos

Bach Party Cruises (Captain Cocktail):
• Up to 14 guests: $950/2hrs
• 15-18 guests: $1,050/2hrs
• 19-22 guests: $1,150/2hrs

Requirements: Drivers 16+ with license. Max 350 lbs/ski. Life jackets included.

Tip: Max 6 jet skis per group. They also offer private party boat cruises — combine both for an epic water day!
